display_name: EXIT A/B (vs badday_flush control): IDENTICAL entry (pc_h1<=-20, nf15>=0, 50-500k mcap, same filters) — the ONLY difference is the exit profile. Tests WIDER STOP + LET-WINNERS-RUN, calibrated from 2590 real trades (winners' median MAE only -1.9%, p90 all-trades MAE -7.8%, p90 peak +22.5% on the 25% we let ride past the old +6% TP). Widened: all early cuts (giveback/pre_stop/never_runner/slow_bleed/fast_bail) pulled from -3..-9 to the -12 hard-stop floor so noise doesn't whipsaw winners; upside uncapped: tp1 +6->+13 selling 30% (was 75%), tp2 +12->+30 selling 30%, trail 2->6pp so the ~40% remainder rides the fat tail. Hypothesis: on a fat-tail book, forfeiting fewer winners to noise-stops + capturing the >+22% tail beats the tight-cap profile. PAPER A/B; compare $/tr + max-drawdown vs badday_flush at n>=30 closes before promoting.
